steps.prompt: Is there a finite verb in this sentence?

hints.Yes — 'Facilis' is a verb: 'Facilis' is an adjective meaning 'easy' — it modifies 'descensus'
hints.Yes — 'descensus' is a verb: 'descensus' is a 4th declension noun meaning 'descent' — not a verb
hints.Yes — 'Averno' is a verb: 'Averno' is a proper noun (Avernus) in the dative/ablative — not a verb

hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'facilis' would be 'facilis' (same form), but here it agrees with nominative 'descensus'
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'facilis' would be 'facilem' — 'Facilis' modifies nominative 'descensus'
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'facilis' would be 'facili' — 'Facilis' modifies nominative 'descensus'

hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'descensus' (4th decl.) would also be 'descensūs' with a long -ū-, but here it is the subject
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'descensus' (4th decl.) would be 'descensum' — this is the nominative form
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'descensus' (4th decl.) would be 'descensū' — this is the nominative form

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'descensūs' has genitive in -ūs
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns ending in -us have genitive in -i — 'descensūs' has genitive in -ūs
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'descensūs' has genitive in -ūs

hints.Nominative: Nominative of 'Avernus' is 'Avernus' — 'Averno' has the -o dative/ablative ending
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'Avernus' would be 'Averni' — 'Averno' ends in -o
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'Avernus' would be 'Avernum' — 'Averno' ends in -o

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'Averni' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'Averni' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'Averni' ends in -i

translation: Easy is the descent to Avernus
